target.title: fix(google-vertex): support production ADC modes
target.body:
## Summary

- Allow native `api: "google-vertex"` configs through model API validation.
- Preserve inherited provider-level `api: "google-vertex"` during inline model materialization.
- Extend the existing Google Vertex ADC helper beyond `authorized_user` files by falling back to `google-auth-library` for production ADC modes, including GKE Workload Identity / metadata-server ADC, `external_account`, `service_account`, Cloud Run, GAE, and Compute Engine.

## Why

OpenClaw already has a native Google Vertex transport: `model.api === "google-vertex"` selects the Vertex transport path. The current ADC preflight and token resolver only accept `authorized_user` credentials files. That preserves the local `gcloud auth application-default login` case, but rejects common production GCP auth shapes before the native Vertex transport can succeed.

Production GCP deployments commonly rely on metadata server ADC, GKE Workload Identity, Workload Identity Federation (`external_account`), or service account credentials rather than a static Gemini API key or a user ADC file. This patch keeps the existing `authorized_user` refresh behavior intact and adds the standard `google-auth-library` ADC chain for the other supported runtime modes.

## Related Upstream Work

This PR is scoped as a production ADC completion for the existing native Google Vertex path, not as a new Vertex provider.

Related work:

- #9729 requested Google Vertex ADC support and was closed as implemented.
- #60860 is a broader Google Vertex provider PR that also touches ADC and endpoint routing.
- #74914 adds `api: "google-vertex"` schema/config support, but is still open. This PR includes the schema allowance and also preserves provider-level inherited `api: "google-vertex"` in `normalizeResolvedTransportApi`, since adding to `MODEL_APIS` alone only covers explicit model-level config.
- #65023 tracks a broader `google-genai` SDK provider direction.

The difference in this PR is that it keeps the existing native Vertex transport path, preserves the current `authorized_user` behavior, and extends ADC resolution to production GCP modes such as GKE Workload Identity / metadata server ADC, `external_account`, `service_account`, Cloud Run, GAE, and Compute Engine. It also includes redacted live behavior proof from a GKE-hosted OpenClaw deployment.

## Evidence

Redacted live proof from a GKE-hosted OpenClaw deployment validated the metadata-server ADC path end to end.

Environment and config facts:

```text
GOOGLE_API_KEY_present = no
GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS_present = no
GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT_present = yes
GOOGLE_CLOUD_LOCATION = us-central1
```

Rendered model config:

```text
models.providers.google.apiKey = "gcp-vertex-credentials"
models.providers.google.models[0].api = "google-vertex"
models.providers.google.models[0].id = "gemini-2.5-pro"
models.providers.google.models[0].baseUrl = "https://us-central1-aiplatform.googleapis.com"
agents.defaults.model = "google/gemini-2.5-pro"
```

Metadata-server access token evidence:

```text
[gke-metadata-server]
Fetching access token for the bound Google service account.
"/computeMetadata/v1/instance/service-accounts/default/token?scopes=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.googleapis.com%2Fauth%2Fcloud-platform" HTTP/200
```

Gateway transport evidence:

```text
[provider-transport-fetch]
[model-fetch] start provider=google api=google-vertex model=gemini-2.5-pro
method=POST
url=https://us-central1-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1/projects/<redacted-project>/locations/us-central1/publishers/google/models/gemini-2.5-pro:streamGenerateContent
timeoutMs=120000 proxy=none policy=custom

[provider-transport-fetch]
[model-fetch] response provider=google api=google-vertex model=gemini-2.5-pro
status=200 elapsedMs=2291 contentType=text/event-stream
```

User-visible outcome:

```text
A user-visible OpenClaw response was returned through the configured channel after the Vertex `streamGenerateContent` request completed with HTTP 200.
```

This proves the patch path did not use `x-goog-api-key` or a static service-account JSON file. The deployment used the `gcp-vertex-credentials` marker and obtained a bearer token from metadata-server ADC for the native Google Vertex transport.

## Test plan

- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
  - Result: passed.
- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts src/config/zod-schema.models.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/model.inline-provider.test.ts`
  - Result: passed 3 Vitest shards after rebasing onto current `origin/main`.
  - `test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts`: 1 test file passed, 12 tests passed.
  - `test/vitest/vitest.runtime-config.config.ts`: 1 test file passed, 1 test passed.
  - `test/vitest/vitest.extension-providers.config.ts`: 1 test file passed, 53 tests passed.
- `git diff --check`
  - Result: passed.

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)
target.author: damianfelixpago
